repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Hey all, I've been keeping this little change under my belt for a couple years and thought it might be time to share with the world. All this change does is add an option to the `fillchars` setting: colorcol. This sets the character that neovim should fill the Color Column with when no other character is present. I like this because I find it more sleek to have a 1px line down my color column (by using '│' as my colorcol char) than to have a whole cell highlighted.

Feel free to accept, ignore, whatever, just thought it was time to bring it forth. This is my first neovim pull request, so be gentle please :)

(screenshot is with fillchars+=colorcol:│)

![colorcol](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3434593/185770890-52fd59ba-ed2f-49d6-a613-17e8c3375a43.png)

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1350,7 +1350,8 @@ A jump table for the options with a short description can be found at |Q_op|.
 'colorcolumn' 'cc'	string	(default "")
 			local to window
 	'colorcolumn' is a comma-separated list of screen columns that are
-	highlighted with ColorColumn |hl-ColorColumn|.  Useful to align
+	highlighted with ColorColumn |hl-ColorColumn| and drawn using the
+	colocol option from 'fillchars'.  Useful to align

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
	  colorcol	' '		character to display in the colorcolumn
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -2478,6 +2479,7 @@ A jump table for the options with a short description can be found at |Q_op|.
 	  diff		'-'		deleted lines of the 'diff' option
 	  msgsep	' '		message separator 'display'
 	  eob		'~'		empty lines at the end of a buffer
+	  colorcol:c	' '		character to display in the colorcolumn

@@ -2515,6 +2517,7 @@ A jump table for the options with a short description can be found at |Q_op|.
 	  fold		Folded			|hl-Folded|
 	  diff		DiffDelete		|hl-DiffDelete|
 	  eob		EndOfBuffer		|hl-EndOfBuffer|
+	  colorcol:c	ColorColumn		|hl-ColorColumn|

comment_regular_data.body: I like this. What do other core members think?
Worth seeing what Bram thinks? Otherwise a small and self-contained change; some bike-shedding on the name maybe, which is a tad long -- `'colcol'`? `'colorc`?
FWIW I personally prefer the more verbose version.

I'm pretty ambivalent to the name, I'm cool with whatever is considered most idiomatic.

I'm having trouble figuring out why the checks are not successful. Are they flaky?

> I'm having trouble figuring out why the checks are not successful. Are they flaky?

No, it's not normal that 5+ CI jobs fail.

```
ERROR    test/functional/ui/highlight_spec.lua @ 834: CursorLine and CursorLineNr highlights overridden by Error, ColorColumn if fg not set
test/functional/helpers.lua:106: Vim(set):E474: Invalid argument: fillchars=colorc:

stack traceback:
	test/functional/helpers.lua:106: in function 'request'
	test/functional/helpers.lua:272: in function 'command'
	test/functional/ui/highlight_spec.lua:859: in function <test/functional/ui/highlight_spec.lua:834>
```

> FWIW I personally prefer the more verbose version.

+1 for colorcol.

Though it would be good to shoot this upstream and see if there's interest.
Fixed the highlight.lua test file and cleaned up the commits.
Since `colorcolumn` is a comma separated list of column to highlight is this going to show that character on each column?

> Since `colorcolumn` is a comma separated list of column to highlight is this going to show that character on each column?

see https://github.com/vim/vim/pull/11860
Looks like this and https://github.com/vim/vim/pull/11860 have stalled.

repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability

labels.description: Need comments/opinions from people familiar with this topic.
labels.name: needs:discussion

title: feat(options): Add the option "colorcol" to the fillchars setting to set a character that neovim should draw at the colorcolumn if no other character is there.
